title: Barbier Coupling Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Barbier coupling reaction describing in situ generation of organometallic reagents in the presence of carbonyl compounds to furnish alcohols after workup. The page notes mechanistic overlap with Grignard chemistry and discusses both concerted and radical stepwise pathways.
notes: Page emphasizes operational simplicity, aqueous compatibility for some metals, and immediate trapping of unstable organometallic species by aldehydes or ketones.

entities.notes: Overview explicitly compares Barbier to Grignard chemistry.
Mechanistic chart presents SET pathway for organometallic generation.
Canonical outcome after carbonyl addition and workup.

similar_existing.transformation_type: in situ organometallic generation from alkyl/allyl halides with direct addition to carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: The Barbier reaction generates an organometallic reagent in the presence of a carbonyl compound so that addition occurs immediately without prior isolation of the Grignard-like species.

similar_existing.transformation_type: beta-keto ester / dicarbonyl cleavage
similar_existing.description_short: Mentioned in color-explanation front matter on page xiv.

similar_existing.transformation_type: ortho formylation of phenols and heteroarenes via dichlorocarbene chemistry
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational dichlorocarbene-based formylation method for phenols and related heteroarenes that usually delivers ortho-aldehydes but can also produce abnormal rearrangement products.

similar_existing.transformation_type: other
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: MVK; CH3CN | MVK; CH3CN / H2O | Mg(ClO4)2; chiral bis-oxazoline ligand; DCM
similar_existing.description_short: Aza-ene reaction

similar_existing.transformation_type: other

title: Birch Reduction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Birch reduction describing dissolving-metal reduction of aromatic rings or electron-poor heteroarenes with alkali metals in liquid ammonia and an alcohol proton source to give unconjugated cyclohexadienes or partially reduced heterocycles. The mechanism proceeds through sequential electron transfer, radical anion formation, protonation, and regiocontrolled second electron/proton delivery.
notes: Highlights substituent-dependent regioselectivity, use of Li or Na in NH3(l), and applicability to pyridines, furans, and related systems under electron-withdrawing substitution.

entities.notes: First electron-transfer intermediate in Birch reduction.
Typical partially reduced arene product class.
Variant that traps reduced intermediates with electrophiles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: dissolving-metal dearomatization / reductive alkylation
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: Li or Na, NH3(l), ROH, t-BuOH, EtOH
similar_existing.description_short: Dissolving-metal reduction of aromatic rings and heteroarenes to unconjugated dienes or partially reduced heterocycles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: CeCl3/NaBH4 reduction of alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones to allylic alcohols
similar_existing.description_short: Selective 1,2-reduction of enones to allylic alcohols using sodium borohydride in the presence of cerium(III) chloride.

similar_existing.transformation_type: other
similar_existing.description_short: The Buchner reaction, involving the thermal or photochemical reaction of ethyl diazoacetate with benzene to form a cycloheptatriene derivative via a norcaradiene intermediate.

similar_existing.transformation_type: aminomethylation of enolizable carbonyls or other CH-acidic partners with formaldehyde and amines
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational carbon–carbon bond-forming reaction that installs aminomethyl substituents onto enolizable carbonyl systems via iminium intermediates.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Lewis-acid-mediated propargylic substitution/decomplexation of cobalt-alkyne complexes
similar_existing.description_short: Cobalt-complex-mediated propargylic substitution method that enables controlled generation and trapping of stabilized propargylic cations.

title: Carroll Rearrangement (Kimel-Cope Rearrangement) – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Carroll rearrangement describing [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of allylic beta-keto esters to gamma,delta-unsaturated ketones, often followed by decarboxylation, and conceptually related to a decarboxylative Claisen rearrangement.
notes: Key concepts: ester enolate, [3,3]-sigmatropic shift, decarboxylation, gamma-delta unsaturated ketone formation.

entities.notes: Canonical substrate class for the Carroll/Kimel-Cope rearrangement.
Concerted rearrangement step explicitly shown on the overview page.
Characteristic product class formed after rearrangement and decarboxylation.

similar_existing.transformation_type: decarboxylative Claisen-type [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: allylic beta-keto esters, ester enolates, heat, LDA, decarboxylation
similar_existing.description_short: Rearrangement of allylic beta-keto esters to gamma,delta-unsaturated ketones via a [3,3]-sigmatropic shift and loss of carbon dioxide.

similar_existing.transformation_type: 1,5-diene [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement / ring expansion of strained dienes
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal reversible [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of 1,5-dienes that reorganizes carbon skeletons, including medium-ring-forming strained-substrate variants.

similar_existing.transformation_type: anionic silyl migration / annulation
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: alkoxide or organolithium generation, acyl silanes, THF
similar_existing.description_short: Anionic migration of silyl groups from carbon to oxygen that enables rearrangement and annulation logic.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement of alpha-diazoketones to ketenes
similar_existing.description_short: Conversion of alpha-diazoketones into ketenes with accompanying 1,2-shift, widely used for ring contraction and homologation chemistry.

similar_existing.transformation_type: 4-carbonyl-substituted oxazole isomerization / heterocycle skeletal editing
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal rearrangement of 4-carbonyl-substituted oxazoles through dicarbonylnitrile ylide intermediates to isomeric oxazole products and related heterocycle-edited scaffolds.

title: Combes Quinoline Synthesis – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Combes quinoline synthesis describing acid-catalyzed condensation of primary aryl amines with beta-diketones or beta-ketoesters through Schiff base formation, enamine isomerization, cyclization, and dehydration to substituted quinolines or 4-hydroxyquinolines.
notes: Key concepts: aryl amine plus beta-dicarbonyl partner, Schiff base intermediates, acid/heat-driven annulation to quinoline frameworks.

entities.notes: Named quinoline annulation family overview.
Imine intermediate explicitly highlighted in the overview mechanism.
Characteristic product class from beta-ketoester variants.

similar_existing.transformation_type: arylamine plus beta-dicarbonyl annulation to quinolines / 4-hydroxyquinolines
similar_existing.description_short: Acid-catalyzed condensation of aryl amines with beta-dicarbonyl compounds to furnish substituted quinolines or 4-hydroxyquinolines through Schiff base intermediates.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-catalyzed indolization of arylhydrazones
similar_existing.description_short: Acid-catalyzed conversion of arylhydrazones into substituted indoles through tautomerization, sigmatropic rearrangement, and ring closure.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-promoted cyclization of N-acyl-o-toluidines or related anilides to indoles
similar_existing.description_short: Classical and modified intramolecular anilide cyclization methods for building 2-substituted and 2,3-disubstituted indoles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd-catalyzed heteroannulation of o-iodoanilines with internal alkynes to indoles
similar_existing.description_short: Palladium-catalyzed annulation of o-iodoanilines with alkynes that provides substituted indoles and fused azaindole-type scaffolds with predictable regioselectivity.

similar_existing.transformation_type: annulative synthesis of 5-hydroxyindoles from benzoquinones and enamine partners
similar_existing.description_short: Benzoquinone-based indole annulation that furnishes substituted 5-hydroxyindoles from amino-enone or enamine partners.

title: Cope Elimination / Cope Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Cope elimination describing oxidation of tertiary amines to N-oxides followed by stereospecific syn elimination upon heating to furnish alkenes and dialkylhydroxylamines through a five-membered cyclic transition state.
notes: Key concepts: tertiary amine N-oxide precursor, syn elimination, alkene formation, thermal beta-hydrogen removal.

entities.notes: Named elimination family overview.
The overview stresses stereospecific syn elimination from amine N-oxides.
Canonical substrate class for the Cope elimination.

similar_existing.transformation_type: tertiary amine N-oxide syn elimination to alkene and hydroxylamine
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal syn elimination of tertiary amine N-oxides to alkenes and dialkylhydroxylamines through a cyclic transition state.

similar_existing.transformation_type: alcohol dehydration via xanthate pyrolysis / syn elimination
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal syn elimination of xanthate esters derived from alcohols to furnish alkenes under rearrangement-free conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: heteroaromatic amination / nucleophilic substitution of hydride
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: NaNH2, KNH2, liquid ammonia, toluene, high temperature, azines or diazines
similar_existing.description_short: Direct amination of electron-deficient nitrogen heteroarenes by amide equivalents through nucleophilic substitution of hydride.

similar_existing.transformation_type: ester enolate acylation to beta-keto esters / Dieckmann cyclization
similar_existing.description_short: Base-mediated condensation of esters through ester enolate acylation to give beta-keto esters, crossed products, or cyclic Dieckmann products.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-mediated conversion of nitriles and alcohols or thiols into imino ether or imino thioether salts and downstream amidines or esters
similar_existing.description_short: Classical nitrile activation method that turns nitriles into imino ether salts and provides a versatile gateway to amidines, esters, and related nitrogen-rich motifs.

title: Cope Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Cope rearrangement describing the reversible thermal [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of 1,5-dienes, including divinylcyclopropane and cyclobutene ring-expansion variants, through chairlike or boatlike concerted transition states.
notes: Key concepts: 1,5-diene substrate class, thermal [3,3]-shift, stereospecific concerted rearrangement, strained-ring variants.

entities.notes: Named [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement family overview.
Core mechanistic descriptor on the overview page.
Canonical substrate class for the rearrangement.

similar_existing.transformation_type: 1,5-diene [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement / ring expansion of strained dienes
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal reversible [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of 1,5-dienes that reorganizes carbon skeletons, including medium-ring-forming strained-substrate variants.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: OHC-COOH H2O | KH, 18-crown-6, ClCO2Me, DTBMP, KOH | (CH2O)n, Na2SO4
similar_existing.description_short: synthesis of N-benzylallylglycine

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-catalyzed migration of a 2,3-epoxy alcohol to an isomeric epoxide; aza/thia variants form aziridino or thiirano alcohols
similar_existing.description_short: Epoxide migration manifold that relays stereochemistry and functional handles across epoxy alcohols and related heteroatom-substituted systems.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement of activated hydroxamic acids to isocyanates and trapped amine/urea/carbamate products
similar_existing.description_short: Activated-hydroxamate rearrangement that furnishes isocyanates under mild conditions and enables access to amines, carbamates, and ureas after trapping.

similar_existing.transformation_type: decarboxylative Claisen-type [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: allylic beta-keto esters, ester enolates, heat, LDA, decarboxylation
similar_existing.description_short: Rearrangement of allylic beta-keto esters to gamma,delta-unsaturated ketones via a [3,3]-sigmatropic shift and loss of carbon dioxide.

title: Corey-Bakshi-Shibata Reduction (CBS Reduction) – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Corey-Bakshi-Shibata reduction describing enantioselective oxazaborolidine-catalyzed borane reduction of ketones to secondary alcohols through face-selective hydride delivery from a chiral catalyst-borane complex.
notes: Key concepts: oxazaborolidine catalyst, borane source, ketone-to-secondary-alcohol reduction, high enantioselectivity.

entities.notes: Named asymmetric reduction family overview.
Chiral boron catalyst class central to the CBS mechanism.
Characteristic product class of ketone CBS reduction.

similar_existing.transformation_type: enantioselective ketone reduction to secondary alcohols
similar_existing.description_short: Catalytic enantioselective reduction of ketones to secondary alcohols using chiral oxazaborolidine catalysts and borane reagents.

similar_existing.transformation_type: amination
similar_existing.description_short: Named reaction family listed in the alphabetical reaction index pages of the source book.

similar_existing.transformation_type: other
similar_existing.description_short: Named reaction family listed in the alphabetical reaction index pages of the source book.

title: Corey-Chaykovsky Epoxidation and Cyclopropanation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Corey-Chaykovsky epoxidation and cyclopropanation describing base-generated sulfoxonium or sulfonium ylides that transfer methylene units to aldehydes, ketones, or alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl compounds to form epoxides or cyclopropanes.
notes: Key concepts: sulfur ylide generation with NaH in DMSO, carbonyl methylene transfer, epoxide versus cyclopropane formation.

entities.notes: Named sulfur ylide transfer family overview.
Shared reactive intermediate for both epoxidation and cyclopropanation branches.
Canonical product class from aldehyde and ketone substrates.

similar_existing.transformation_type: sulfur ylide methylene transfer to carbonyls or enones forming epoxides or cyclopropanes
similar_existing.description_short: Sulfur ylide transfer chemistry that converts carbonyl compounds into epoxides and enones into cyclopropanes under basic conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: allylic alcohol/alkene -> optically active cyclopropane
similar_existing.description_short: Color-explanation page family seed from page xiv.

similar_existing.transformation_type: methylene cyclopropanation of alkenes
similar_existing.description_short: Cyclopropanation of alkenes by zinc carbenoid methylene transfer with strong stereospecificity and useful allylic-alcohol direction.

title: Corey-Kim Oxidation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Corey-Kim oxidation describing conversion of primary and secondary alcohols to aldehydes or ketones with N-chlorosuccinimide, dimethyl sulfide, and triethylamine via alkoxysulfonium salt intermediates under mild low-temperature conditions.
notes: Key concepts: NCS/DMS activation, Corey-Kim reagent formation, alkoxysulfonium intermediates, mild alcohol oxidation.

entities.notes: Named mild alcohol oxidation family overview.
Key reactive intermediate highlighted in the mechanistic scheme.
Representative oxidation product class from primary alcohol substrates.

similar_existing.transformation_type: primary/secondary alcohol oxidation to aldehyde or ketone
similar_existing.description_short: Mild oxidation of alcohols to aldehydes or ketones using the NCS/DMS/Et3N reagent system via alkoxysulfonium intermediates.

similar_existing.transformation_type: TPAP/NMO oxidation of primary and secondary alcohols to aldehydes and ketones
similar_existing.description_short: Mild ruthenate-catalyzed alcohol oxidation that converts sensitive primary and secondary alcohols into aldehydes and ketones using TPAP and NMO.

similar_existing.transformation_type: vicinal diol oxidative cleavage to carbonyl fragments
similar_existing.description_short: Oxidative cleavage of vicinal diols to carbonyl compounds using lead tetraacetate, often as a mild alternative for advanced cyclic diol fragmentation.

similar_existing.transformation_type: activated aromatic aldehyde or ketone to substituted phenol
similar_existing.description_short: Oxidation/rearrangement of activated aromatic aldehydes or ketones to substituted phenols through peroxide addition and aryl migration.

similar_existing.transformation_type: chromium(VI)-mediated oxidation of alcohols to carbonyl compounds or carboxylic acids
similar_existing.description_short: Chromium-based oxidation platform encompassing Jones reagent and related Cr(VI) reagents for the conversion of alcohols and related substrates into carbonyl compounds.

title: Corey-Nicolaou Macrolactonization – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Corey-Nicolaou macrolactonization describing activation of hydroxy acids as 2-pyridinethiol esters using 2,2'-dipyridyl disulfide and triphenylphosphine, followed by intramolecular lactonization to medium- and large-ring lactones under high-dilution conditions.
notes: Key concepts: 2-pyridinethiol ester activation, dipolar intermediate, intramolecular lactonization, medium- and large-ring lactone formation.

entities.notes: Named macrolactonization family overview.
Activated acyl intermediate central to the Corey-Nicolaou protocol.
Canonical product class explicitly shown in the overview scheme.

similar_existing.transformation_type: hydroxy acid cyclization to medium- or large-ring lactone
similar_existing.description_short: Macrolactonization method that activates hydroxy acids as 2-pyridinethiol esters before intramolecular closure to medium- and large-ring lactones.

similar_existing.transformation_type: intramolecular macrolactonization of hydroxy acids to medium- and large-ring lactones using carbodiimide activation with DMAP/DMAP.HCl or EDCI
similar_existing.description_short: Macrolactonization protocol for cyclizing hydroxy acids to macrocycles using carbodiimide activation and DMAP/DMAP.HCl mediation under high-dilution conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: high-dilution macrolactonization of hydroxy acids to medium or large ring lactones
similar_existing.description_short: Macrocyclization method that converts hydroxy acids into medium- and large-ring lactones via mixed anhydrides and DMAP-catalyzed intramolecular acyl transfer.

title: De Mayo Cycloaddition (Enone-Alkene [2+2] Photocycloaddition) – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the De Mayo cycloaddition describing photochemical [2+2] cycloaddition between enones and alkenes to generate cyclobutanes or, via beta-hydroxy ketone intermediates and retro-aldol fragmentation, 1,5-diketones. The page emphasizes triplet-state exciplexes, 1,4-biradicals, and topology-dependent regioselectivity.
notes: Key concepts: triplet enone photochemistry, enone-alkene [2+2] cycloaddition, beta-hydroxy ketone intermediates, retro-aldol fragmentation, substituted cyclobutane formation.

entities.notes: Named photochemical enone-alkene [2+2] family overview.
Triplet-state encounter complex invoked before 1,4-biradical formation.
Canonical direct product class of the enone-alkene [2+2] photocycloaddition.

similar_existing.transformation_type: photochemical enone-alkene [2+2] cycloaddition with optional retro-aldol fragmentation
similar_existing.description_short: Photochemical [2+2] cycloaddition between enones and alkenes that can furnish cyclobutanes directly or 1,5-diketones after retro-aldol fragmentation.

similar_existing.transformation_type: concerted [4+2] cycloaddition of diene and dienophile to cyclohexene derivatives
similar_existing.description_short: Concerted [4+2] cycloaddition that rapidly generates six-membered rings with predictable stereo- and regioselectivity in normal or inverse electron-demand modes.

similar_existing.transformation_type: [2+2] cycloaddition of ketenes with alkenes, imines, or carbonyls
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal or Lewis acid assisted [2+2] cycloaddition chemistry of ketenes that furnishes four-membered rings.

similar_existing.transformation_type: electron-rich heterodiene [4+2] cycloaddition to oxygenated cyclohexenes or dihydropyrones
similar_existing.description_short: Cycloaddition chemistry of Danishefsky's diene that rapidly builds oxygenated or heteroatom-containing six-membered rings from carbonyl, quinone, or imine partners.

similar_existing.transformation_type: heteroatom-containing Diels-Alder [4+2] cycloaddition
similar_existing.description_short: Pericyclic or stepwise hetero-[4+2] annulation used to assemble densely functionalized oxygen- and nitrogen-heterocycles.

title: Demjanov and Tiffeneau-Demjanov Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Demjanov and Tiffeneau-Demjanov rearrangements describing nitrous-acid-mediated ring expansion of aminomethylcycloalkanes or 1-aminomethylcycloalkanols to the corresponding cycloalkanol or cycloalkanone homologues through diazonium-ion formation and 1,2-alkyl migration.
notes: Key concepts: nitrosation to diazonium ions, ring expansion by 1,2-alkyl shift, Demjanov versus Tiffeneau-Demjanov substrate classes, competitive rearrangement pathways.

entities.notes: Named ring-expansion family overview.
Key rearrangement step following diazonium-ion formation and nitrogen loss.
Representative ring-expanded product class produced in the Tiffeneau-Demjanov variant.

similar_existing.transformation_type: nitrous-acid-mediated ring expansion of aminomethylcycloalkanes or aminoalcohols to larger-ring alcohol/ketone homologues
similar_existing.description_short: Nitrosation-triggered ring-expansion chemistry of aminomethylcycloalkanes and aminoalcohols that furnishes cycloalkanol or cycloalkanone homologues through 1,2-shift rearrangement.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement
similar_existing.description_short: Named reaction family listed in the alphabetical reaction index pages of the source book.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement of vicinal diols or β-heterosubstituted alcohols to carbonyl compounds via cationic [1,2]-migration
similar_existing.description_short: Rearrangement manifold that converts vicinal diols or activated β-heterosubstituted alcohols into carbonyl compounds through a cationic [1,2]-shift and is widely used for ring expansion and stereocontrolled skeletal editing.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-, base-, or photochemically induced alkyl migration in cyclohexadienones to substituted phenols
similar_existing.description_short: Rearrangement of cyclohexadienones to substituted phenols driven by rearomatization, often proceeding through sequential 1,2-shifts and sensitive to substitution pattern and reaction conditions.

title: Dess-Martin Oxidation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Dess-Martin oxidation describing Dess-Martin periodinane (DMP), a soluble hypervalent iodine(V) reagent derived from IBX, as a mild and selective oxidant for 1° and 2° alcohols and oximes to the corresponding carbonyl compounds.
notes: Key concepts: IBX to DMP reagent preparation, hypervalent iodine oxidation, mild neutral conditions, aldehyde/ketone formation, alkoxyperiodinane intermediates.

entities.notes: Named hypervalent-iodine oxidation family overview.
Soluble iodine(V) oxidant central to the named reaction.
Canonical aldehyde/ketone product class formed from alcohol or oxime oxidation.

similar_existing.description_short: Mild and chemoselective oxidation using Dess-Martin periodinane, a soluble hypervalent iodine(V) reagent derived from IBX, to convert alcohols and related substrates into carbonyl compounds.

similar_existing.description_short: Named oxidation that converts silyl enol ethers into conjugated enones or enals under Pd(II) conditions.

title: Dieckmann Condensation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Dieckmann condensation describing intramolecular Claisen condensation of diesters to cyclic beta-keto esters and the related Thorpe-Ziegler cyclization of dinitriles to enamino nitriles under strong base.
notes: Key concepts: intramolecular Claisen condensation, cyclic beta-keto ester formation, Thorpe-Ziegler cyclization, reversible enolate-driven ring closure, ring-size effects.

similar_existing.description_short: Intramolecular Claisen condensation of diesters that yields cyclic beta-keto esters, often complemented by related nitrile cyclizations in ring-building synthesis.

title: Enyne Metathesis – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for enyne metathesis describing catalyst-controlled redistribution of carbon–carbon multiple bonds between an alkene and an alkyne to form conjugated 1,3-dienes, including ring-closing and cross-enyne metathesis manifolds.
notes: Key concepts: Grubbs-type ruthenium carbene catalysis, ring-closing versus cross-enyne modes, 1,n-enyne substrates, and mechanistic divergence through metallacyclobutene intermediates.

entities.notes: Named alkene–alkyne metathesis family overview.

similar_existing.description_short: Alkene metathesis redistributes C=C bonds through metal-carbene and metallacyclobutane intermediates and encompasses RCM, CM, ROM, ROMP, and ADMET manifolds.

similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: RCM catalyst, benzene or DCM; H2 / Pd(C); BBr3, DCM | Grubbs catalyst; PPTS, acetone / H2O | Grubbs catalyst
similar_existing.description_short: Ring-Closing Metathesis (RCM)

title: Eschenmoser Methenylation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for Eschenmoser methenylation describing installation of an exocyclic methylene equivalent at the alpha-position of carbonyl compounds by reaction of enolates with Eschenmoser’s salt followed by elimination from the resulting aminomethylated intermediate.
notes: Key concepts: enolate trapping with Eschenmoser’s salt, formation of alpha-(dimethylamino)methyl carbonyl intermediates, oxidative or Hofmann/Cope-type elimination, and access to alpha-methylenated ketones or lactones.

entities.notes: Named alpha-methylenation family overview.
Characteristic methylene-transfer reagent highlighted on the overview page.
Canonical product class formed after elimination from the aminomethylated intermediate.

similar_existing.transformation_type: alpha-methylenation of carbonyl compounds via aminomethylation and elimination
similar_existing.description_short: Method for converting enolizable carbonyl compounds into exocyclic methylene products by enolate capture of Eschenmoser’s salt followed by elimination from the resulting aminomethyl intermediate.

similar_existing.transformation_type: reductive N-methylation of amines
similar_existing.description_short: One-pot reductive methylation of primary or secondary amines using formaldehyde and formic acid, often without overalkylation to quaternary ammonium salts.

similar_existing.transformation_type: fragmentation of epoxyketone hydrazones to alkynones / alkynals
similar_existing.description_short: Fragmentation of epoxy ketone hydrazones that furnishes alkynones or alkynals through a nitrogen-expelling Grob-type process.

similar_existing.transformation_type: alkene -> cyclopropane
similar_existing.description_short: Color-explanation page family seed from page xiv.

similar_existing.transformation_type: other
similar_existing.description_short: The Buchner reaction, involving the thermal or photochemical reaction of ethyl diazoacetate with benzene to form a cycloheptatriene derivative via a norcaradiene intermediate.

title: Eschenmoser-Claisen Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Eschenmoser-Claisen rearrangement describing Claisen-type [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of allylic or benzylic alcohols heated with N,N-dimethylacetamide dimethyl acetal to furnish gamma,delta-unsaturated amides through ketene N,O-acetal intermediates.
notes: Key concepts: allylic alcohol exchange with DMA-DMA, ketene aminal / ketene N,O-acetal formation, chairlike or boatlike transition-state control, and stereoselective generation of gamma,delta-unsaturated amides.

entities.notes: Named amide-Claisen rearrangement family overview.
Reactive ketene aminal / ketene N,O-acetal intermediate shown in the mechanism.
Characteristic rearrangement product class generated from allylic alcohol precursors.

similar_existing.transformation_type: allylic alcohol to gamma,delta-unsaturated amide rearrangement
similar_existing.description_short: Claisen-type rearrangement that converts allylic alcohols into gamma,delta-unsaturated amides under thermal conditions using DMA-DMA.

similar_existing.transformation_type: allyl ether to allyl carbonyl or allyl phenol [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: allyl vinyl ether|allyl aryl ether|heat|Lewis acid variants
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of allyl vinyl and allyl aryl ethers to gamma,delta-unsaturated carbonyl compounds or allyl phenols.

similar_existing.transformation_type: thermal Claisen rearrangement of allylic alcohols to gamma,delta-unsaturated esters
similar_existing.description_short: Claisen-type rearrangement of allylic alcohols and ortho esters that installs gamma,delta-unsaturated ester motifs with predictable alkene geometry.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: [3,3] | 1. LHMDS, toluene, -78 °C; 2. 120 °C, 6h | CH3CH2COCl; K2CO3, Me3Al; CHCl3, 0 °C
similar_existing.description_short: Amide enolate induced aza-Claisen rearrangement in the synthesis of fluvircinine A1

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement of activated hydroxamic acids to isocyanates and trapped amine/urea/carbamate products
similar_existing.description_short: Activated-hydroxamate rearrangement that furnishes isocyanates under mild conditions and enables access to amines, carbamates, and ureas after trapping.

title: Eschenmoser-Tanabe Fragmentation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Eschenmoser-Tanabe fragmentation describing conversion of tosylhydrazones of alpha,beta-epoxy ketones into cyclic alkynones, alkynals, or acyclic alkynones by base-, acid-, or heat-induced fragmentation with loss of nitrogen.
notes: Key concepts: epoxyketone hydrazone formation, Grob-type fragmentation, release of N2 and sulfinate leaving groups, and formation of medium-sized cyclic or acyclic alkynes / alkynones from epoxy ketone precursors.

entities.notes: Named epoxyketone fragmentation family overview.
Medium-sized cyclic alkynone product class emphasized in the overview scheme.
Open-chain alkyne-containing product class accessible from epoxy ketone hydrazones.

title: Eschweiler-Clarke Methylation (Reductive Alkylation) – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for Eschweiler-Clarke methylation describing one-pot reductive methylation of primary or secondary amines with formaldehyde and formic acid, together with related reductive alkylation and cyclization variants using carbonyl compounds and reducing agents.
notes: Key concepts: iminium formation from amine and formaldehyde, hydride transfer from formic acid, selective N-methylation without overalkylation to quaternary salts, and relationship to Leuckart-Wallach-type reductive alkylation.

entities.notes: Named reductive methylation family overview.
Related reductive alkylation family explicitly compared on the overview page.
Canonical tertiary-amine product class formed under Eschweiler-Clarke conditions.
